channel.title: Walking cross the paths
channel.description: Recent content on Walking cross the paths

item.title: Guest - Swave
item.description:
Advice:

  • Contact the factory/suppliers as early as possible to know cost, current customer, etc.
  • About iterating without a technical background: find right people for right thing

item.title: Ideation: Use Kano model
item.description:
Speaker: Winnie Leung

  • Go wild. Think out of the box.
  • Goal: have many ideas to solve the problem.
  • Measure qualitative outcome
  • Aim for good enough, not perfect.
  • Create value for customers.

Kano Model

  • Goals: Organize expressed and non-expressed customer needs

    • Categories
      • dissatisfiers (expected quality, must-have)
      • satisfiers (Normal quality)
      • delighters (exciting quality, wows)

  • [illustration graph]

  • Use user research data to support your categorization!

item.title: Product Architecture
item.description:
My take-away:

  • Highlight innovative function in design

item.title: Value Proposition
item.description:
Value Proposition elements

  • Who your product/service is for
  • What benefits
  • Why not competitors

Value Proposition Canvas

  • Customer: Job, Pain, Gain
  • Product: Products and services, Pain relievers, Gain creators

item.title: Problem Definition II
item.description:
Finding the right problem

[insert slides later]

Design Probes

Better uncovering people’s action than their opnion

Types can be:

  • Logging material
  • Cameras
  • Prototypes e.g.,
  1. to-go probe kits
  2. low-fidelity prototypes
    • testing the problem space (how people will use it, more UX?), not a solution
    • e.g., test the size of product, e.g., a phone
  3. Role play when you are designing a service
    • Will find, say, if the size is too small or too big, will the user use it intuitively

What to look for?

Anything weird or intersting!

item.title: Vibe Coding Design Style
item.description:
Source (in Chinese): https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV1EpdoBFE3Z

Summary:

  1. Colour
    • Muted palette 柔和颜色
    • Monochromatic 单一颜色的
  2. Layout
    • Card based design with layered elements 有纵深的卡片式设计
  3. General style
    • Neo-minimalism
  4. Design philosophy
    • Approachable sohistication

item.title: Guest Arex
item.description:
CEO外包不了的三件事

  • 找方向
    • 原型机/概念图在用户研究时重要
    • 测渠道（线上/线下），用户兴趣度（email转化率）
  • 找人
    • 团队基因对齐
    • 广结善缘
    • 抓住一切机会
    • 庆祝正反馈，复盘负反馈
  • 找钱（融资）
    1. 筛选融资机构
    2. 先见less fit，从负反馈中迭代
    3. 迭代得差不多之后pitch more fit
    4. 融资过程：投资人（无决策权）- 合伙人 - 投资意向书 - 合同（需要法务，SPA和SHA） - 打钱（结束）
      • 融资是体力活，融资当面试
      • 不要把过程当结果（钱入账前都不算）

一些建议

  • 拒绝伪需求，识别真需求（投流etc）
  • 用户-研发-产品循环。不要等到完美产品再问用户
  • 多向前辈请教

item.title: Problem Definition
item.description: Back up with user research data.

item.title: User Research I
item.description:
Speaker: Bryan Guo

User research is important!

The top 1 reason why start-ups fail is no market need.

Advices

Talk to real users in the market to test your assumption about their need.
How?

  • Qualitative - know why: 1-on-1 interview, focus group, etc.
  • Quantitative - test your hypothesis:

User research method

Usability Testing: Early feedback on product design (after prototype comes out) A/B Testing: Select the best concept. Test the conversion rate 转化率

item.title: Guest Tarran
item.description:
Speaker: Ben Guo
Key takeaways:

  1. Think globally: market, think big
  2. Surround Yourself with Teachers & Friends
  3. Move Fast and Break things.
  4. Be the best.
  5. For hardware, try lots of versions concurrently in one iteration and pick one best

item.title: Assets Mapping
item.description:
1. How to approach a problem? How to identify opportunity or pain point?
  • Think divergently & reframe. e.g., watch vs portable time device
  • Levels of solution: incremental or disruptive or systemic innovation
  1. Key design concepts
    1. Think holisticly
    2. Make abstract ideas tangible
    3. Approach problem in a human-center manner
    4. Design collaboratively
    5. Design for simplicity
  2. Community Assets Map: Financial capital, cultural capital, social capital, etc.
  3. Map the assets and problems/opportunities

item.title: Sources
item.description:
• CSDI (Common Spatial Data Infrastructure): https://portal.csdi.gov.hk/csdi-webpage/
    information infrastructure to share spatial data of Hong Kong, supporting various smart city applications

item.title: Design Thinking I
item.description:
By Prof. Joseph Chan

Advice for maximizing InboX value

  • Reach out for resources proactively: Work space, personnel, industry connection
  • Be open-minded about
    • Project topic: Embrace changes from time to time
    • Team members: No competition in the camp. 7/10 entered the science park incumbation programme. Goal is to develop a incumbation-ready level.
  • Think about product value - to customers, to investors
  • Prototype quickly and iteratively
  • Empathy: Understand what customers real need
    • Positive need prompts consumption more than negative need. e.g., swimming
    • Product hits
  • Think about what is your core tech.

Two diamond diagram for design

Pitch Deck Structure

A sample is like this:
Hi! Here is my notes about the 10th InnoX Camp 2026.
